HEARING ON CLAIM OF EXEMPTION, PROOF OF SERVICE (TRANSACTION ID # 64345115) FILED BY PLAINTIFF US BANK NATIONAL ASSOCIATION HEARING SET FOR NOV-21-2019 AT 09:30 AM IN DEPT 302

LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT CYNTHIA ONETO'S CLAIM OF EXEMPTION IS GRANTED IN PART AND DENIED IN PART. THE LEVYING OFFICER IS DIRECTED TO RELEASE $200.00 OF THE FUNDS HELD TO THE JUDGMENT CREDITOR FOR PAYMENT ON THE JUDGMENT AND TO RELEASE THE BALANCE OF THE FUNDS HELD TO THE JUDGMENT DEBTOR. DEFENDANT'S FUTURE PAYCHECKS WILL BE GARNISHED ONCE PER MONTH IN THE AMOUNT OF $200.00 ON THE FIRST PAYCHECK OF EACH MONTH. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: ETHAN P. SCHULMAN; CLERK: GINA GONZALES; COURT REPORTER: CHRISTINE JORDAN, CSR#12262, EMAIL: CJORDAN@SFTC.ORG

calendar.matters:
HEARING ON CLAIM OF EXEMPTION
Off Calendar Nov-21-2019 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Steve Levy, Esq. of the Law Offices of Steve Levy, T:800.637.6922, for plaintiff/moving party, by Court Call;
Cynthia Oneto, T:415.713.5403, defendant/judgment debtor/claimant, appearing on her own behalf.

RULING: Hearing held. The parties were questioned by the Court as to the amount and source of the funds held, the defendant's financial situation and capacity to pay, etc. The court passed the matter and ordered the parties to meet and confer in order to reach an agreement.

The matter was re-called. Pursuant to the parties' stipulation, the Court ruled as follows:

DEFENDANT CYNTHIA ONETO'S CLAIM OF EXEMPTION is granted in part and denied in part. The levying officer is directed to release $200.00 of the funds held to the judgment creditor for payment on the judgment and to release the balance of the funds held to the judgment debtor. Defendant's future paychecks will be garnished once per month in the amount of $200.00 on the first paycheck of each month.

Order signed in open court. The clerk shall serve the order on all parties, including a certified copy to the San Francisco Sheriff's Office.

Judge: Ethan P. Schulman; Clerk: Gina Gonzales; Court Reporter: Christine Jordan, CSR#12262, email: cjordan@sftc.org
